title: Global Markets Face New Doubts Over AI Spending
subtitle: Investors question if massive investments in artificial intelligence will ever lead to real profits

summary.en: Global stock markets recently experienced a sharp decline as investors grew worried about the high costs of artificial intelligence. Technology shares, particularly those of chipmakers, saw significant losses after months of record-breaking growth. Analysts are now debating whether the current AI trend is a sustainable business model or a dangerous market bubble. Many companies are under pressure to prove that their expensive projects can generate actual income. As the market remains volatile, experts are closely watching upcoming corporate earnings reports for signs of stability.
summary.zh: 全球股市近期經歷了劇烈下跌，因為投資人對人工智慧的高昂成本感到擔憂。科技股，特別是晶片製造商，在經歷了數月的破紀錄成長後損失慘重。分析師目前正在辯論當前的人工智慧趨勢究竟是可持續的商業模式，還是危險的市場泡沫。許多公司面臨壓力，必須證明其昂貴的專案能夠產生實際收入。由於市場持續波動，專家們正密切關注即將發布的企業財報，以尋找市場趨於穩定的跡象。

paragraphs.en: Global financial markets are facing a period of high uncertainty as a wave of selling hits major technology stocks. After months of rapid growth, investors are beginning to question whether the massive spending on artificial intelligence (AI) is truly worth the cost. This shift in mood has caused significant drops in the Nasdaq index and affected chipmakers across the globe, leading many to wonder if the recent "AI boom" has finally lost its momentum.
paragraphs.zh: 全球金融市場正處於高度不確定的時期，一波拋售潮正衝擊著主要的科技股。在經歷了數月的快速成長後，投資人開始質疑在人工智慧（AI）上的巨額支出是否真的物有所值。這種情緒的轉變導致那斯達克指數大幅下跌，並波及全球晶片製造商，讓許多人懷疑近期的「人工智慧熱潮」是否終於後繼無力。

paragraphs.en: According to BBC Business, the tech-heavy Nasdaq index fell by about 3% as investors moved away from companies that had previously seen their share prices double since 2022. This sudden change follows a long period where optimism about AI pushed stock markets to unprecedented highs. However, as the market cools, analysts are now asking a difficult question: can the actual use of AI by businesses really justify such expensive stock prices?
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司商業新聞（BBC Business）報導，以科技股為主的那斯達克指數下跌了約百分之三，投資人紛紛撤離那些自二零二二年以來股價翻倍的公司。這種突如其來的轉變，發生在市場對人工智慧的樂觀情緒將股市推向空前高點的一段長時間之後。然而，隨著市場冷卻，分析師們現在提出了一個棘手的問題：企業對人工智慧的實際應用，真的能證明如此昂貴的股價是合理的嗎？

paragraphs.en: This concern is not limited to the United States. The Guardian reports that the nervousness has spread to international markets, with significant declines seen in Asia. In South Korea, major chipmakers like Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix saw their shares drop by over 12% in a single day. Meanwhile, Japan’s Nikkei 225 index also fell by 3.5%. These drops reflect a growing fear that the global tech sector has relied too heavily on a small group of companies to drive market value.
paragraphs.zh: 這種擔憂不僅限於美國。《衛報》（The Guardian）報導指出，這種不安情緒已蔓延至國際市場，亞洲股市出現了顯著跌幅。在南韓，三星電子（Samsung Electronics）與海力士（SK Hynix）等主要晶片製造商的股價在一天內下跌超過百分之十二。與此同時，日本日經二二五指數也下跌了百分之三點五。這些跌幅反映出市場日益擔憂全球科技產業過度依賴少數幾家公司來拉抬市場價值。

paragraphs.en: One of the most debated topics is the sheer amount of money being poured into AI infrastructure. NPR News noted that over $580 billion was invested globally in AI over the past year, adding to more than $1 trillion spent in the four years before that. Mark Vena, CEO of SmartTech Research, explained that the market is trying to understand if these massive investments will ever start to see returns. This uncertainty has led some experts to compare the current situation to the "dot-com bubble" of the early 2000s, where high expectations for internet companies eventually led to a market crash.
paragraphs.zh: 最受爭議的話題之一，是投入人工智慧基礎設施的龐大資金。美國全國公共廣播電台（NPR News）指出，過去一年全球在人工智慧領域的投資超過五千八百億美元，加上此前四年的投資，總額已超過一兆美元。智慧科技研究公司（SmartTech Research）執行長馬克·維納（Mark Vena）解釋說，市場正試圖了解這些巨額投資是否能開始獲得回報。這種不確定性導致一些專家將當前局勢與二零零零年代初期的「網路泡沫」相提並論，當時對網路公司過高的期望最終導致了市場崩盤。

paragraphs.en: Specific companies are feeling the pressure more than others. Micron Technology, a major producer of memory chips, saw its shares fall by over 13% on Tuesday. Similarly, Google-parent Alphabet experienced its worst market performance in over a year following the departure of key AI researchers. Even SpaceX, which recently went public, has faced a difficult start. After its initial public offering (IPO) — the first time a company sells shares to the public — the firm saw its price drop significantly as investors worried about the high costs of its ongoing projects.
paragraphs.zh: 特定公司感受到的壓力比其他公司更大。記憶體晶片大廠美光科技（Micron Technology）週二股價下跌超過百分之十三。同樣地，谷歌（Google）母公司字母公司（Alphabet）在關鍵人工智慧研究人員離職後，經歷了超過一年來最糟糕的市場表現。即使是近期上市的太空探索科技公司（SpaceX）也面臨艱難的開局。在首次公開募股（IPO）——即公司首次向大眾出售股票——之後，由於投資人擔心其進行中專案的高昂成本，該公司的股價大幅下跌。

paragraphs.en: Analysts are currently divided on what this means for the future. Some, like Vivek Arya from Bank of America, remain optimistic. Arya suggested in a note to clients that the current market movement is a standard reaction after a period of historic growth. He believes that as companies solve physical infrastructure and power issues, the sector will continue to see strong demand. In this view, the recent sell-off is simply a healthy pause rather than the start of a long-term decline.
paragraphs.zh: 分析師目前對於這對未來意味著什麼看法分歧。一些人，如美國銀行（Bank of America）的維維克·阿亞（Vivek Arya）仍保持樂觀。阿亞在給客戶的報告中指出，目前的市場波動是經歷歷史性成長後的標準反應。他認為，隨著企業解決實體基礎設施與電力問題，該產業將持續看到強勁需求。從這個角度來看，近期的拋售僅是健康的停頓，而非長期衰退的開始。

paragraphs.en: However, other experts are more cautious. Many argue that the era of easy market gains is over, especially as corporations begin to look more closely at their IT budgets. With the Federal Reserve signaling that it may increase interest rates — the cost of borrowing money — the environment for tech companies is becoming more challenging. Higher interest rates make it more expensive for firms to fund their expensive AI projects through debt.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，其他專家則較為謹慎。許多人認為，市場輕鬆獲利的時代已經結束，特別是當企業開始更嚴格審視其資訊科技預算時。隨著美國聯準會（Federal Reserve）暗示可能提高利率——即借貸資金的成本——科技公司的環境正變得更具挑戰性。較高的利率使得企業透過債務來資助昂貴的人工智慧專案變得更加昂貴。

paragraphs.en: As the trading week continues, all eyes are on upcoming corporate earnings reports. Tech giants are now under immense pressure to prove that their AI investments are generating real profits rather than just marketing buzz. Whether this period of volatility is a temporary setback or the beginning of a larger retreat remains the central question for investors worldwide. For now, the market remains in a state of nervous waiting, looking for any sign that the AI revolution can deliver on its promise of long-term growth.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著交易週持續進行，所有目光都集中在即將發布的企業財報上。科技巨頭現在面臨巨大壓力，必須證明他們的人工智慧投資正在創造真正的利潤，而不僅僅是行銷炒作。這段波動期究竟是暫時的挫折，還是更大規模撤退的開始，仍是全球投資人最關心的核心問題。目前，市場仍處於焦慮的等待狀態，尋找任何人工智慧革命能夠兌現其長期成長承諾的跡象。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become less active, intense, or enthusiastic.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（涼爽的），這裡作動詞，形容市場熱度下降。文中：However, as the market cools, analysts are now asking a difficult question: can the actual use of AI by businesses really justify such expensive stock prices?

keyTerms.definitionEn: To invest or spend a large amount of money into something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思是「倒進去」，這裡指大量資金投入某個領域。文中：One of the most debated topics is the sheer amount of money being poured into AI infrastructure.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To offer a company's shares for sale to the public for the first time.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個商業慣用語，指公司首次公開發行股票。文中：Even SpaceX, which recently went public, has faced a difficult start.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To do what you promised to do.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「傳送」，這裡指實現承諾。文中：For now, the market remains in a state of nervous waiting, looking for any sign that the AI revolution can deliver on its promise of long-term growth.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Tech stocks tumble on concerns over AI spending

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: US AI stock sell-off shakes markets from Wall Street to Asia

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: Is AI 'one big bubble'? Behind the tech sell-off

previousArticles.title: Global Markets Shaken as AI Investment Concerns Grow
previousArticles.summaryEn: Global stock markets have experienced a sudden drop as investors worry about the high costs of AI technology. Major technology companies are facing pressure to prove that their expensive AI projects will actually make money. Some analysts compare the current situation to the dot-com bubble of the early 2000s. Meanwhile, companies like Oracle are cutting jobs to fund their massive infrastructure needs. The future of the tech sector remains uncertain as experts debate whether this is a temporary pause or a larger problem.

previousArticles.title: The Workforce Gap: Who Will Fill the Jobs?
previousArticles.summaryEn: This article explores the global labor shortage caused by aging populations. It examines how this trend impacts key industries like manufacturing and services. Finally, it highlights the challenges governments and businesses face in maintaining economic stability.

quiz.question: What specific impact did the current market shift have on the South Korean technology sector?

options.A: The Nasdaq index fell by 3% in sympathy with South Korean firms.
options.B: Major chipmakers experienced a share price decline of more than 12% in one day.
options.C: Companies like Samsung and SK Hynix were forced to stop their AI infrastructure spending.
options.D: The South Korean market outperformed the Japanese Nikkei 225 index.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，文中明確提到在南韓，大型晶片製造商如三星電子 (Samsung Electronics) 和 SK 海力士 (SK Hynix) 的股價在一天之內下跌了超過 12%。選項 A 錯誤，因為 3% 的跌幅是指 Nasdaq 指數，而非南韓市場。選項 C 錯誤，文章並未提到這些公司停止投資。選項 D 錯誤，文中僅提到兩者皆下跌，並未比較誰表現較好。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might rising interest rates be particularly damaging to AI-focused tech companies?

options.A: Because high interest rates discourage individual investors from buying IPO shares.
options.B: Because tech companies rely on government subsidies that decrease when interest rates rise.
options.C: Because these companies often depend on borrowed money to finance their expensive AI development projects.
options.D: Because the Federal Reserve has explicitly ordered tech companies to stop their AI research.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第七段，聯準會升息會增加借貸成本，而文中提到科技公司需要透過債務來資助其昂貴的 AI 專案，因此升息會使這些專案的融資變得更加困難。選項 A 雖然與股市有關，但並非文中解釋升息影響的邏輯。選項 B 錯誤，文中未提到政府補貼。選項 D 錯誤，聯準會並未下令停止研究，這屬於過度推論。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the phrase 'marketing buzz' imply about the current state of AI investments?

options.A: The investments are based on genuine, proven financial profits.
options.B: The investments are driven more by hype and public excitement than by actual, tangible results.
options.C: The investments are being used primarily to fund advertising campaigns for new products.
options.D: The investments have successfully created a stable and quiet market environment.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在文中最後一段，作者提到科技巨頭需證明其投資產生了「real profits」（真實利潤）而非僅是「marketing buzz」。在這裡，「buzz」指的是由行銷炒作出來的熱度或話題，暗示這些投資目前可能缺乏實質的獲利支撐，僅是市場上的熱門討論。選項 A 與文意相反。選項 C 將「行銷」一詞過度字面解讀。選項 D 與文章強調的「波動性」(volatility) 與「不確定性」矛盾。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the current global tech market?

options.A: The AI revolution has officially ended, and investors are moving their money into traditional manufacturing.
options.B: Global markets are experiencing a temporary correction caused by a lack of interest in technology.
options.C: There is growing uncertainty and debate over whether the massive spending on AI will lead to actual long-term profitability.
options.D: The 'dot-com bubble' has already repeated itself, and a major global financial crash is inevitable.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心在於探討市場對於 AI 高額投資是否能轉化為真實獲利的懷疑與不安。文章從市場下跌、專家觀點到企業財報的壓力，皆圍繞此主題展開。選項 A 過於絕對，文中僅提到「疑慮」而非「革命結束」。選項 B 錯誤，市場並非對科技失去興趣，而是對「投資回報」感到懷疑。選項 D 錯誤，文中僅提到專家將現況與 dot-com 泡沫「比較」，並未斷言泡沫已經發生或崩盤不可避免。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
